Title: Redox-mediated activation of ATG3 promotes ATG8 lipidation and autophagy progression in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes autophagosome formation and autophagy progression under oxidative stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein abundance increased under ROS-linked autophagy-activating conditions: norflurazon treatment, cerulenin treatment, methyl viologen treatment, and high-light stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redox-mediated reduction/activation of ATG3 promotes ATG8 lipidation and autophagy progression; oxidation inhibits ATG8 lipidation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrATG3 undergoes reversible redox-dependent monomerization/oxidation with midpoint redox potential about -311.9 mV. 2. The catalytic Cys255 is the key redox-sensitive residue required for dimerization behavior and for ATG8 lipidation activity. 3. Reduced CrATG3 enhances ATG8-PE formation in cell-free extracts, whereas oxidizing conditions abolish lipidation. 4. CrATG3 protein abundance increases and its redox state dynamically shifts during ROS-linked stress, becoming fully reduced when ATG8 activation is highest.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein redox assays; cysteine-to-serine mutagenesis; redox titration; cell-free ATG8 lipidation assay; in vivo alkylation/redox western blot; immunoblotting under stress conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Reducing agents (DTTred, GSH); oxidants (H2O2, Cu2+, DTTox); norflurazon; cerulenin; methyl viologen; high light
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focuses on autophagy lipidation machinery rather than classical nutritional metabolite accumulation. ATG3 acts on ATG8 conjugation to phosphatidylethanolamine. Dimeric ATG3 was readily detected in vitro but not convincingly in vivo.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate stress-responsive autophagy/redox homeostasis in microalgae, though direct nutrient-quality application is not established.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ScATG3 supports ATG8 lipidation, while oxidation suppresses lipidation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScATG3 is reversibly redox regulated with midpoint redox potential about -312.9 mV. 2. Catalytic Cys234 is required for redox-sensitive dimerization. 3. Under reducing conditions, recombinant ScATG3 restores or enhances ATG8 lipidation in yeast extracts, including atg3 mutant extracts. 4. Oxidizing conditions prevent ATG8 lipidation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein redox assays; catalytic cysteine mutagenesis; redox titration; yeast cell-free lipidation assay using WT and atg3 mutant extracts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Yeast was used mainly for evolutionary conservation of ATG3 redox control, not for nutrient-metabolism trait analysis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Reductive activation of target protein through disulfide reduction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Reduces oxidized ATG3 and promotes its monomeric active form
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports autophagosome formation under oxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently reduces CrATG3, thereby enabling ATG8 lipidation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant CrTRXh1 efficiently reduces and monomerizes oxidized CrATG3 in vitro. 2. This supports a role for thioredoxin in maintaining ATG3 in its active reduced state. 3. The study proposes TRXh1 as part of the redox control mechanism linking oxidative stress to autophagy.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro reduction assay with recombinant proteins and nonreducing SDS-PAGE
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low DTTred used to maintain TRXh1 in reduced form
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent activator of ATG3 redox state and thus ATG8 lipidation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified here as a direct biochemical regulator of ATG3; prior proteomics had suggested ATG3 as a thioredoxin target.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering redox-responsive autophagy in microalgae
